If the new mean of the marks is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>2</mn></math>. then their new variance is equal to:

So,

∑xi=10n   ....i

Also,

Also,

Now, new variance is

=∑xi2-82+12220-10.22

=2080+4×2020-104.04

=108-104.04

=3.96

q: If the sum of the deviations of <math><mn>50</mn></math> observations from <math><mn>30</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn></math>, then the mean of these observations is :
question: If the sum of the deviations of <math><mn>50</mn></math> observations from <math><mn>30</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn></math>, then the mean of these observations is :
solution:
Let the observations be x1, x2, ..., x50.

Given x1-30+x2-30+.....+x50-30=50

⇒ x1+x2+.....+x50=50+30×50=1550

Now, Mean =x1+x2+....+x5050=155050=31.

If two observations are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the variance of the remaining <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> observations is :
question: The mean and variance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn></math> observations are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>16</mn></math> respetively. If two observations are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the variance of the remaining <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> observations is :
solution:
Let a, b, c, d, e be 5 unknown observations.

Given that No. of observation, n=7, Mean =8, Variance =16

∴ sum of observations  =No. of observation ×Mean=7×8=56

⇒ Mean of 5 remaining observations =56-8-65=425

As, we know that,

Variance for 7 observations=Σxi2n-x¯2

⇒16=Σxi27-64

⇒Σxi2=560

⇒a2+b2+c2+d2+e2+64+36=560

⇒a2+b2+c2+d2+e2=460

Variance of remaining 5 observations =Σxi2n-x¯2=4605-4252=53625

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $\mathrm{X}=\{x \in \mathrm{~N}: 1 \leq x \leq 19\}$ and for some $a, b \in \mathbb{R}, \mathrm{Y}=\{a x+b: x \in \mathrm{X}\}$. If the mean and variance of the elements of Y are 30 and 750, respectively, then the sum of all possible values of $b$ is
question: Let $\mathrm{X}=\{x \in \mathrm{~N}: 1 \leq x \leq 19\}$ and for some $a, b \in \mathbb{R}, \mathrm{Y}=\{a x+b: x \in \mathrm{X}\}$. If the mean and variance of the elements of Y are 30 and 750, respectively, then the sum of all possible values of $b$ is
solution:
Mean of $X = \frac{1+19}{2} = 10$, Variance of $X = \frac{19^2-1}{12} = 30$.
For $Y = aX + b$: Mean of $Y = 10a + b = 30$ and Variance of $Y = a^2 \cdot 30 = 750$.
$a^2 = 25 \Rightarrow a = 5$ or $a = -5$.
If $a = 5$: $b = 30 - 50 = -20$.
If $a = -5$: $b = 30 + 50 = 80$.
Sum of all possible values of $b = -20 + 80 = 60$.

If <math><mn>5</mn></math> of the observations are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>12</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>14</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the product of the remaining two observations is
question: The mean and variance for seven observations are <math><mn>8</mn></math> and <math><mn>16</mn></math> respectively. If <math><mn>5</mn></math> of the observations are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>12</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>14</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the product of the remaining two observations is
solution:
Let the remaining two observations are x and y.

Mean  x¯=∑xiN=2+4+10+12+14+x+y7

⇒ 42+x+y7=8

⇒x+y=14     ...1
Variance, 

    σ2=∑xi2N-x¯2=4+16+100+144+196+x2+y27-64 

⇒σ2=460+x2+y27-64=16

⇒x2+y2=100    …2

From 1 and 2, we get,

x=6, y=8 and xy=48

⇒n+12n+16-n+122=10

var2,4,6….,2m=16⇒var1,2….,m=4

subject: Mathematics
q:
Let $\mathrm{x}_1, \mathrm{x}_2, \ldots \ldots, \mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{n}}$ be $\mathrm{n}$ observations, and let $\overline{\mathrm{x}}$ be their arithematic mean and $\sigma^2$ be their variance. 
Statement $1$: Variance of $2 x_1, 2 x_2, \ldots \ldots, 2 x_n$ is $4 \sigma^2$.
Statement $2$: Arithmetic mean of $2 \mathrm{x}_1, 2 \mathrm{x}_2, \ldots . ., 2 \mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{n}}$ is $4 \overline{\mathrm{x}}$.
question:
Let $\mathrm{x}_1, \mathrm{x}_2, \ldots \ldots, \mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{n}}$ be $\mathrm{n}$ observations, and let $\overline{\mathrm{x}}$ be their arithematic mean and $\sigma^2$ be their variance. 
Statement $1$: Variance of $2 x_1, 2 x_2, \ldots \ldots, 2 x_n$ is $4 \sigma^2$.
Statement $2$: Arithmetic mean of $2 \mathrm{x}_1, 2 \mathrm{x}_2, \ldots . ., 2 \mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{n}}$ is $4 \overline{\mathrm{x}}$.
options: Statement $1$ is false, statement $2$ is true
Statement $1$ is true, statement $2$ is true; statement $2$ is a correct explanation for statement $1$
Statement $1$ is true, statement $2$ is true; statement $2$ is not a correct explanation for statement $1$
Statement $1$ is true, statement $2$ is false
solution:
$\sigma^2=\sum \frac{x_i^2}{n}-\left(\sum \frac{x_i}{n}\right)^2$
Variance of $2 x_1, 2 x_2, \ldots . ., 2 x_n=\sum \frac{\left(2 x_i\right)^2}{n}-\left(\sum \frac{2 x_i}{n}\right)^2=4\left[\sum \frac{x_i^2}{n}-\left(\sum \frac{x_i}{n}\right)^2\right]=4 \sigma^2$ Statement $1$ is true.
A.M. of $2 x_1, 2 x_2, \ldots \ldots, 2 x_n=\frac{2 x_1+2 x_2+\cdots+2 x_n}{n}=2\left(\frac{x_1+x_2+\cdots+x_n}{n}\right)=2 \bar{x}$
Statement $2$ is false.

solution:

7,10,13-- & 8,10,12,--- ∈ A.P

rth term of an A.P ⇒ tr = a +r-1d

Here a = 7, d = 3 & a = 8, d =2

tr =3r +4  &  tr =2r +6

∑r=110(3r+4)(2r+6)
=∑r=1106r2+26r+24=6∑r=110r2+26∑r=110r+∑r=11024

∑r=1n1 = n, ∑r=1nr = nn+12, ∑r=1nr2 = nn+12n+16, ∑r=1nr3 = nn+122

=6 10×11×216+2610×112+2410
=2310+1430+240=3980
mean =398

q: The number of values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>N</mi></math> such that the variance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>7</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>12</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>43</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>a</mi></math> is a natural number is:
question: The number of values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>N</mi></math> such that the variance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>7</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>12</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>43</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>a</mi></math> is a natural number is:
solution:
Calculating Mean =3+7+12+a+43-a5=13

Variance =9+49+144+a2+43-a25-132∈N

⇒2a2-a+15=n (where n∈N)

⇒2a2-a+1-5n=0 must have solution as natural numbers

So, D=40n-7 always has 3 at unit place for any value of n

⇒D can't be perfect square

So, a can't be integer.

q: Let the mean of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn></math> observations is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> and the standard deviation is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math>. However, one observation was wrongly recorded. The sum of the correct and incorrect observations is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>70</mn></math>. If the mean of the correct set of observations is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>16</mn></math>, then the variance of the correct set is equal to
question: Let the mean of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn></math> observations is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> and the standard deviation is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math>. However, one observation was wrongly recorded. The sum of the correct and incorrect observations is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>70</mn></math>. If the mean of the correct set of observations is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>16</mn></math>, then the variance of the correct set is equal to
solution:
By using the formula of mean we get, =∑xi50=15⇒∑xi=750

Now variance=∑xi250-152=22⇒∑xi2=11450

⇒∑xinew=50×16=800

So, ∑xinew-∑xi=800-750=50

Hence, if wrong observation was x then corrected one is x+50.
⇒x+x+50=70⇒x=10

Therefore, the correct observation =60.

Now, ∑xi2new=11450-102+602=14950

Therefore, variance of the correct set =1495050-162=299-256=43.

q: Let the mean and the variance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> observation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>68</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>44</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>48</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>60</mn></math> be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>55</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>194</mn></math>, respectively if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>></mo><mi>b</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>b</mi></math> is
question: Let the mean and the variance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> observation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>68</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>44</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>48</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>60</mn></math> be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>55</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>194</mn></math>, respectively if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>></mo><mi>b</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>b</mi></math> is
solution:
Mean, x=a+b+68+44+48+606=55

⇒a+b+220=330

⇒a+b=110   ...i

Variance, σ2=∑xi-x2n

⇒σ2=a-552+b-552+132+112+72+526=194

⇒a-552+b-552+364=1164

⇒110-b-552+b-552=800

⇒255-b2=800

⇒55-b=20

⇒b=35

⇒a=75

⇒a+3b=75+105=180

subject: Mathematics
q: If both the mean and the standard deviation of <math><mn>50</mn></math> observations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>50</mn></msub></math> are equal to <math><mn>16</mn></math>, then the mean of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>50</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is
question: If both the mean and the standard deviation of <math><mn>50</mn></math> observations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>50</mn></msub></math> are equal to <math><mn>16</mn></math>, then the mean of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>…</mo><mo>,</mo><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>50</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is
solution:
For observations x1, x2, ...........x50

We have, mean, x¯=∑xi 50=16   .....i

And, standard deviation, σ=∑xi250-x¯2=16

⇒∑xi250=162+x¯2

⇒∑xi250=162+162=512  .....ii

Now, the mean value of x1-42, x2-42, ..., x50-42 will be

=∑xi-4250=∑xi2-8 ∑xi+16×5050

=∑xi250-8∑xi50+16

 Using i and ii,

=512-8×16+16=400.

subject: Mathematics
q: If the mean deviation about the median of the numbers $\mathrm{a}, 2 \mathrm{a}, \ldots, 50 \mathrm{a}$ is 50 , then $|\mathrm{a}|$ equals
question: If the mean deviation about the median of the numbers $\mathrm{a}, 2 \mathrm{a}, \ldots, 50 \mathrm{a}$ is 50 , then $|\mathrm{a}|$ equals
solution:
$\frac{1}{\mathrm{n}} \sum\left|\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}-\mathrm{A}\right|$
$\mathrm{A}=$ Median $=\frac{25 \mathrm{a}+26 \mathrm{a}}{2}=25.5 \mathrm{a}$
Mean deviation $=\frac{1}{50}\{|\mathrm{a}-25.5 \mathrm{a}|+|2 \mathrm{a}-25.5 \mathrm{a}|\}=\frac{2}{50}\{(24.5 \mathrm{a}+23.5 \mathrm{a})+\ldots(0.5 \mathrm{a})\}$
$=\frac{2}{50}\{312.5 \mathrm{a}\}=50 \quad$ (Given)
$\Rightarrow 625 \mathrm{a}=2500 \Rightarrow \mathrm{a}=4$

subject: Mathematics
q:
If the variance of the frequency distribution
$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline x & c & 2 c & 3 c & 4 c & 5 c & 6 c \\
\hline f & 2 & 1 & 1 & 1 & 1 & 1 \\
\hline
\end{array}$
is 160, then the value of $c \in N$ is
question:
If the variance of the frequency distribution
$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline x & c & 2 c & 3 c & 4 c & 5 c & 6 c \\
\hline f & 2 & 1 & 1 & 1 & 1 & 1 \\
\hline
\end{array}$
is 160, then the value of $c \in N$ is
solution:
$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline \mathrm{x} & \mathrm{C} & 2 \mathrm{C} & 3 \mathrm{C} & 4 \mathrm{C} & 5 \mathrm{C} & 6 \mathrm{C} \\
\hline \mathrm{f} & 2 & 1 & 1 & 1 & 1 & 1 \\
\hline
\end{array}$
$\bar{x}=\frac{(2+2+3+4+5+6) C}{7}=\frac{22 C}{7}$
$\begin{aligned} & \operatorname{Var}(x)=\frac{\mathrm{c}^2\left(2+2^2+3^2+4^2+5^2+6^2\right)}{7} \\ & -\left(\frac{22 \mathrm{c}}{7}\right)^2 \\ & =\frac{92 \mathrm{c}^2}{7}-\mathrm{c}^2 \times \frac{484}{49} \\ & =\frac{(644-484) \mathrm{c}^2}{49}=\frac{160 \mathrm{c}^2}{49} \\ & 160=\frac{160 \times \mathrm{c}^2}{49} \Rightarrow \mathrm{c}=7\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: If the standard deviation of the numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>-</mo><mn>1,</mn><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>k</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mn>5</mn></msqrt></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mi>k</mi></math> is equal to
question: If the standard deviation of the numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>-</mo><mn>1,</mn><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>k</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mn>5</mn></msqrt></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mi>k</mi></math> is equal to
solution:
S.D.=∑xi2n-∑xin2=5

⇒∑xi2n-∑xin2=5

⇒1+1+0+k24--1+0+1+k42=5

⇒2+k24-k216=5 

⇒8+4k2-k216=5

⇒8+3k2=80

⇒3k2=72 

⇒k2=24 

⇒k=±26

Since, k>0

 ⇒k=26.

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>9</mn></munderover><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mi>i</mi></msub><mo>−</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>9</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>9</mn></munderover><msup><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mi>i</mi></msub><mo>−</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>45</mn></math>, then the standard deviation of the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math> items <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>9</mn></msub></math> is
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>9</mn></munderover><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mi>i</mi></msub><mo>−</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>9</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>9</mn></munderover><msup><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>x</mi><mi>i</mi></msub><mo>−</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>45</mn></math>, then the standard deviation of the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math> items <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>9</mn></msub></math> is
solution:
Given ∑ i=1 9 x i −45=9

⇒∑i=19xi=54

Also ∑i=19xi2−10xi+25=45

⇒∑i=19xi2−10×54+25×9=45

⇒∑xi2=45+540−225

=360
∴ σ2=∑xi2N−∑xiN2

= 360 9 − ( 54 9 ) 2

So, variance =40−36=4

∴ SD=2

q: In a set of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>n</mi></math> distinct observations, each of the observation below the median of all the observations is increased by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> and each of the remaining observations is decreased by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math>. Then, the mean of the new set of observations :
question: In a set of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>n</mi></math> distinct observations, each of the observation below the median of all the observations is increased by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> and each of the remaining observations is decreased by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math>. Then, the mean of the new set of observations :
solution:
New mean=nΣxi+5n+nΣyi-3n2n

=nΣxi+nΣyi2n+2n2n

=old mean +2n2n

=old mean+1

subject: Mathematics
q: Let the mean and standard deviation of marks of class A of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn></math> students be respectively <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mfenced><mrow><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>, and the mean and standard deviation of marks of class <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> students be respectively <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>55</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>α</mi></math>. If the mean and variance of the marks of the combined class of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi></math> students are respectively <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>350</mn></math> , then the sum of variances of classes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> is
question: Let the mean and standard deviation of marks of class A of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn></math> students be respectively <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mfenced><mrow><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>, and the mean and standard deviation of marks of class <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> students be respectively <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>55</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>α</mi></math>. If the mean and variance of the marks of the combined class of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi></math> students are respectively <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>350</mn></math> , then the sum of variances of classes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> is
solution:
Let the mean is denoted by xi¯, variance by σi2, so standard deviation will be σi, now as per given data we get,

A B A+Bx¯1=40 x¯2=55 x¯=50σ1=α σ2=30-α σ2=350n1=100 n2=n 100+n

So, using the formula of mean in A+B we get,

x¯=100×40+55n100+n=50

⇒  n=200

Now using the formula of variance we get,

σ12=∑xi2100-402=α2

And σ22=∑yi2200-552=30-α2

So, variance of A+B will be,

σ2=∑xi2+∑yi2300-502=3502

⇒3502=1600+α2×100+3025+30-α2×200300-502

⇒α2-40α+300=0

⇒α=10  or 30

And α=30 is not possible

So, σ1+2σ2=2102+202=500

subject: Mathematics
q: The mean of a data set comprising of 16 observations is 16. If one of the observation value 16 is deleted and three new observations valued 3, 4 and 5 are added to the data, then the mean of the resultant data is
question: The mean of a data set comprising of 16 observations is 16. If one of the observation value 16 is deleted and three new observations valued 3, 4 and 5 are added to the data, then the mean of the resultant data is
solution:
Given, ( ∑ i=1 15 x i )+16 16 =16

⇒ ∑i=115xi +16= 256

⇒∑i=115xi=240

Required mean =∑i = 115xi+3+4+518=240+3+4+518

=25218=14

subject: Mathematics
q: The first of the two samples in a group has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn></math> items with mean <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> and standard deviation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If the whole group has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>250</mn></math> items with mean <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>6</mn></math> and standard deviation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mn>13</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>44</mn></msqrt><mo>,</mo></math> then the standard deviation of the second sample is:
question: The first of the two samples in a group has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn></math> items with mean <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> and standard deviation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If the whole group has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>250</mn></math> items with mean <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>6</mn></math> and standard deviation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mn>13</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>44</mn></msqrt><mo>,</mo></math> then the standard deviation of the second sample is:
solution:
We have, number of terms in the first group n1=100 with their mean X1=15 and number of terms in whole group is n1+n2=250 and their mean X=15.6.

Variance of first groupV1x=9 and whole group is Varx=13.44.

Now, σ2=n1σ12+n2σ22n1+n2+n1n2n1+n22x¯1-x¯22

and n2=150,x¯2=16,V2(x)=σ2

Therefore, 13.44=100×9+150×σ22250+100×1502502×1

⇒σ22=16

⇒σ2=4

subject: Mathematics
q: The mean of five observations is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> and their variance is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9.20</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If three of the given five observations are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1,</mn><mo> </mo><mn>3</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then a ratio of other two observations is
question: The mean of five observations is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> and their variance is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9.20</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If three of the given five observations are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1,</mn><mo> </mo><mn>3</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then a ratio of other two observations is
solution:
Let, the two unknown terms are x & y.

The mean is defined as μ=∑xiN

⇒5=1+3+8+x+y5

⇒25=12+x+y

⇒x+y=13    ...1

And, the variance is defined as σ2=∑xi-μ2N=∑xi2N-μ2

⇒9.2=1+9+64+x2+y25-25

⇒34.2×5=74+x2+y2

⇒171=74+x2+y2

⇒97=x2+y2     ...2

Put the value of y from equation 1 and put in 2, to get

97=x2+13-x2

⇒97=x2+169-26x+x2

⇒2x2-26x+72=0

⇒x2-13x+36=0

⇒x-4x-9=0

⇒x=4 or x=9

And, y=9 or y=4 respectively.

Hence, the ratio of x : y is 4 : 9 or 9 : 4.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Let the mean and variance of the frequency distribution

x: x1=2 x2=6 x3=8 x4=9
f: 4 4 α β

be 6 and 6.8 respectively. If x3 is changed from 8 to 7, then the mean for the new data will be:
question:
Let the mean and variance of the frequency distribution

x: x1=2 x2=6 x3=8 x4=9
f: 4 4 α β

be 6 and 6.8 respectively. If x3 is changed from 8 to 7, then the mean for the new data will be:
solution:
Given

x: x1=2 x2=6 x3=8 x4=9 Total
f: 4 4 α β 8+α+β
fx: 8 24 8α 9β 32+8α+9β
fx-x2: 64 0 4α 9β 64+4α+9β

x=∑i=14fixi∑i=14fi

32+8α+9β=8+α+β×6

⇒2α+3β=16       ...1

Also, for the variance, σ2=∑fixi-x2 i=1                       4                       ∑fi i=1    4      

64+4α+9β=8+α+β×6.8

⇒640+40α+90β=544+68α+68β

⇒28α-22β=96

⇒14α-11β=48     ...2

from 1&2

α=5&β=2

so, new mean =32+35+1815=8515=173

subject: Mathematics
q: In an experiment with 15 observations on $x$, the following results were available: $\Sigma x^2=2830, \Sigma x=170$ One observation that was 20 was found to be wrong and was replaced by the correct value 30 . The corrected variance is
question: In an experiment with 15 observations on $x$, the following results were available: $\Sigma x^2=2830, \Sigma x=170$ One observation that was 20 was found to be wrong and was replaced by the correct value 30 . The corrected variance is
solution:
$\Sigma x=170, \Sigma x^2=2830$ increase in $\Sigma x=10$, then
$\Sigma x^{\prime}=170+10=180$
Increase in $\Sigma \mathrm{x}^2=900-400=500$ then
$\Sigma x^{\prime 2}=2830+500=3330$
Variance $=\frac{1}{n} \Sigma x^{\prime 2}-\left(\frac{1}{n} \Sigma x^{\prime}\right)^2$
$=\frac{1}{15} \times 3330-\left(\frac{1}{15} \times 180\right)^2=222-144=78$

subject: Mathematics
q: In a class of 100 students there are 70 boys whose average marks in a subject are 75. If the average marks of the complete class is 72, then what is the average of the girls?
question: In a class of 100 students there are 70 boys whose average marks in a subject are 75. If the average marks of the complete class is 72, then what is the average of the girls?
solution:
Total student $=100 ;$ for 70 stds $75 \times 70=5250 \Rightarrow 7200-5250=1950$
$$
\text { Average of girls }=\frac{1950}{30}=65
$$
